evals.prompt: We're building a new social platform for creators. Think Twitter meets Reddit — users can post, follow, vote, comment. We expect to start with ~5K users and grow to maybe 500K in the first year. What architecture should we use?
evals.expected_output: Should ask clarifying questions before recommending architecture. Should NOT dump a full architecture doc immediately. Should be conversational and present tradeoffs.

assertions.name: asks_clarifying_questions
assertions.description: Response should ask at least 2 clarifying questions before recommending
assertions.check: Contains question marks indicating clarifying questions about requirements

assertions.name: does_not_prescribe_immediately
assertions.description: Should NOT immediately dump a full architecture document with component diagrams
assertions.check: Does not start with a complete architecture diagram or exhaustive tech stack list

assertions.name: mentions_scale_awareness
assertions.description: Should acknowledge that 5K-500K is a specific scale tier and tailor advice accordingly
assertions.check: References the startup/growth scale tier and suggests appropriate simplicity

assertions.name: presents_tradeoffs
assertions.description: Should present at least one tradeoff or comparison between approaches
assertions.check: Contains comparison language (vs, tradeoff, alternative, on the other hand)

evals.prompt: We're debating whether to use fan-out on write or fan-out on read for our feed system. Some of our users will be brands with millions of followers. What should we consider?
evals.expected_output: Should provide deep technical comparison of fan-out strategies with specific numbers. Should mention the hybrid approach (Twitter's pattern). Should reference real-world examples.

assertions.name: explains_both_approaches
assertions.description: Should explain both fan-out on write and fan-out on read with tradeoffs
assertions.check: Covers both approaches with pros and cons

assertions.name: mentions_hybrid
assertions.description: Should mention the hybrid approach as a solution for the celebrity/brand problem
assertions.check: Contains 'hybrid' approach or describes combining both strategies

assertions.name: references_real_platforms
assertions.description: Should reference how Twitter or other platforms handle this
assertions.check: Mentions Twitter, X, or other real platforms as examples

assertions.name: addresses_million_follower_problem
assertions.description: Should specifically address the millions-of-followers scenario mentioned in the prompt
assertions.check: Discusses write amplification or celebrity problem with large follower counts

evals.prompt: We're at about 2M MAU right now and spending $15K/month on AWS. Our CEO wants to cut costs by 40%. Where should we look?
evals.expected_output: Should ask what services they're using before making specific recommendations. Should provide concrete cost-saving strategies with estimated savings percentages. Should mention reserved instances, Graviton, spot instances, caching optimization.

assertions.name: asks_about_current_stack
assertions.description: Should ask what AWS services they're currently using before recommending cuts
assertions.check: Asks about current infrastructure breakdown or AWS services in use

assertions.name: provides_specific_savings
assertions.description: Should mention specific cost-saving strategies with approximate savings percentages
assertions.check: Contains specific percentages or dollar amounts for potential savings

assertions.name: mentions_reserved_instances
assertions.description: Should mention reserved instances or savings plans as a key lever

assertions.name: actionable_recommendations
assertions.description: Recommendations should be specific and actionable, not vague
assertions.check: Contains specific service names and actions (e.g., switch to Graviton, use spot for workers)
